Skip to contents
nemo 0.0.3.9016
  • Intro
    • Installation
    • Structure
    • Schema Walkthrough
    • Schema Table
    • Contribute
    • UML Diagram
    • CI/CD Workflow
    • Devnotes
  • NEWS
  • Reference

Structure

Source: vignettes/structure.Rmd
structure.Rmd

{nemo} is built on top of R’s R6 encapsulated object-oriented programming implementation, which helps with code organisation. It consists of several base classes like Config, Tool, and Workflow which we describe below. Each R6 class can contain public and private functions and non-functions (fields).

Other R packages like {tidywigits} and {dracarys} can create their own Tool and Workflow children classes that inherit (or override) functions from the {nemo} parent classes. This allows for custom parsers and tidiers for specific bioinformatic tools and workflows.

Here we use the Tool1 and Workflow1 classes as examples to illustrate the structure of the package.

Config

A Config object reads a single schema.yaml file from inst/config/tools/<tool>/. Each table entry defines its file pattern, file type, description, and a list of columns — each column carrying both its raw name (as produced by the tool) and its tidy name (snake_case), type, description, and the versions array listing every tool version the column appears in. Config derives versioned raw and tidy views from this unified source on demand. See ?Config.

tool <- params$tool
workflow <- params$workflow
conf <- Config$new(tool, pkg = "nemo")
conf
## #--- Config nemo::tool1 ---#
## 
## |var  |value |
## |:----|:-----|
## |tool |tool1 |
## |pkg  |nemo  |
## |nraw |12    |

Patterns are used to fish out the relevant files from a directory listing.

conf$get_patterns() |>
  knitr::kable(caption = glue("{tool} file patterns."))
Tool1 file patterns.
name pattern
table1 .tool1.table1.tsv$
table2 .tool1.table2.tsv$
table3 .tool1.table3.tsv$
table4 .tool1.table4.tsv$
table6 .tool1.table6.csv$
table5 .tool1.table5.csv$

Descriptions are usually derived from the individual tool documentation.

conf$get_descriptions() |>
  knitr::kable(caption = glue("{tool} file descriptions."))
Tool1 file descriptions.
name description
table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).
table2 Table2 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).
table3 Table3 for tool1 (txt-keyvalue: no header, 2 cols, col1=key col2=value).
table4 Table4 for tool1 (txt-nohead: no header, positional cols X1..XN).
table6 Table6 for tool1 (csv: header present, comma-delimited).
table5 Table5 for tool1 (csv-nohead-long: no header, long format with metric name col).

The versions array on each column lists every tool version that column appears in, giving a full picture of additions and removals across versions.

conf$get_schemas_all() |>
  dplyr::select("name", "version") |>
  dplyr::distinct() |>
  knitr::kable(caption = glue("{tool} schema versions."))
Tool1 schema versions.
name version
table1 v1.2.3
table1 v4.5.6
table1 latest
table2 v1.0.0
table2 latest
table3 v1.0.0
table3 latest
table4 v1.0.0
table4 latest
table6 v1.0.0
table6 latest
table5 latest

get_schemas_all() derives a versioned view from the unified column list. Pass "raw" (default) or "tidy" to get column names in either form:

(s_raw <- conf$get_schemas_all("raw"))
## # A tibble: 12 × 4
##    name   tbl_description                                                           version schema  
##    <chr>  <chr>                                                                     <chr>   <list>  
##  1 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v1.2.3  <tibble>
##  2 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v4.5.6  <tibble>
##  3 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    latest  <tibble>
##  4 table2 Table2 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v1.0.0  <tibble>
##  5 table2 Table2 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    latest  <tibble>
##  6 table3 Table3 for tool1 (txt-keyvalue: no header, 2 cols, col1=key col2=value).  v1.0.0  <tibble>
##  7 table3 Table3 for tool1 (txt-keyvalue: no header, 2 cols, col1=key col2=value).  latest  <tibble>
##  8 table4 Table4 for tool1 (txt-nohead: no header, positional cols X1..XN).         v1.0.0  <tibble>
##  9 table4 Table4 for tool1 (txt-nohead: no header, positional cols X1..XN).         latest  <tibble>
## 10 table6 Table6 for tool1 (csv: header present, comma-delimited).                  v1.0.0  <tibble>
## 11 table6 Table6 for tool1 (csv: header present, comma-delimited).                  latest  <tibble>
## 12 table5 Table5 for tool1 (csv-nohead-long: no header, long format with metric na… latest  <tibble>
s_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$name == "table1", .data$version == "v1.2.3") |>
  dplyr::select("schema") |>
  tidyr::unnest("schema")
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   field      type 
##   <chr>      <chr>
## 1 SampleID   c    
## 2 Chromosome c    
## 3 Start      i    
## 4 End        i    
## 5 metricX    d
(s_tidy <- conf$get_schemas_all("tidy"))
## # A tibble: 12 × 4
##    name   tbl_description                                                           version schema  
##    <chr>  <chr>                                                                     <chr>   <list>  
##  1 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v1.2.3  <tibble>
##  2 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v4.5.6  <tibble>
##  3 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    latest  <tibble>
##  4 table2 Table2 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v1.0.0  <tibble>
##  5 table2 Table2 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    latest  <tibble>
##  6 table3 Table3 for tool1 (txt-keyvalue: no header, 2 cols, col1=key col2=value).  v1.0.0  <tibble>
##  7 table3 Table3 for tool1 (txt-keyvalue: no header, 2 cols, col1=key col2=value).  latest  <tibble>
##  8 table4 Table4 for tool1 (txt-nohead: no header, positional cols X1..XN).         v1.0.0  <tibble>
##  9 table4 Table4 for tool1 (txt-nohead: no header, positional cols X1..XN).         latest  <tibble>
## 10 table6 Table6 for tool1 (csv: header present, comma-delimited).                  v1.0.0  <tibble>
## 11 table6 Table6 for tool1 (csv: header present, comma-delimited).                  latest  <tibble>
## 12 table5 Table5 for tool1 (csv-nohead-long: no header, long format with metric na… latest  <tibble>
s_tidy |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$name == "table1", .data$version == "v1.2.3") |>
  dplyr::select("schema") |>
  tidyr::unnest("schema")
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   field      type 
##   <chr>      <chr>
## 1 sample_id  c    
## 2 chromosome c    
## 3 start      i    
## 4 end        i    
## 5 metric_x   d

Tool

Tool is the main organisation class for all file parsers and tidiers. It contains functions for parsing and tidying typical CSV/TSV files (with column names), and TXT files where the column names are missing. Currently it utilises the very simple readr::read_delim function from the {readr} package that reads all the data into memory. See ?Tool.

These simple parsers are used in 80-90% of cases, so in the future we can optimise the parsing if needed with faster packages such as {data.table}, {duckdb-r}/{duckplyr} or {r-polars}.

We can have different Tool children classes that inherit (or override) functions and fields from the Tool parent class. For example, we can create a Tool object for Tool1 as follows:

  • Initialise a Tool1 object:
tool1_path <- system.file("extdata/tool1", package = "nemo")
t1 <- nemo::Tool1$new(path = tool1_path)
# each class comes with a print function
t1
## #--- Tool nemo::tool1 ---#
## 
## |var     |value                                                                     |
## |:-------|:-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
## |name    |tool1                                                                     |
## |path    |/home/runner/miniconda3/envs/pkgdown_env/lib/R/library/nemo/extdata/tool1 |
## |files   |12                                                                        |
## |tidied  |FALSE                                                                     |
## |written |FALSE                                                                     |
  • Its Config object is also constructed based on the name supplied - this is used internally to find files of interest and infer their schemas:
t1$config
## #--- Config nemo::tool1 ---#
## 
## |var  |value |
## |:----|:-----|
## |tool |tool1 |
## |pkg  |nemo  |
## |nraw |12    |
t1$config$get_patterns()
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   name   pattern                   
##   <chr>  <chr>                     
## 1 table1 "\\.tool1\\.table1\\.tsv$"
## 2 table2 "\\.tool1\\.table2\\.tsv$"
## 3 table3 "\\.tool1\\.table3\\.tsv$"
## 4 table4 "\\.tool1\\.table4\\.tsv$"
## 5 table6 "\\.tool1\\.table6\\.csv$"
## 6 table5 "\\.tool1\\.table5\\.csv$"
t1$get_raw_schema("table1", v = "v1.2.3")
## # A tibble: 5 × 3
##   version field      type 
##   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>
## 1 v1.2.3  SampleID   c    
## 2 v1.2.3  Chromosome c    
## 3 v1.2.3  Start      i    
## 4 v1.2.3  End        i    
## 5 v1.2.3  metricX    d
# t1$get_raw_schema("table1", v = "latest") # default
t1$get_tidy_schema("table1", v = "v1.2.3")
## # A tibble: 5 × 3
##   version field      type 
##   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>
## 1 v1.2.3  sample_id  c    
## 2 v1.2.3  chromosome c    
## 3 v1.2.3  start      i    
## 4 v1.2.3  end        i    
## 5 v1.2.3  metric_x   d
# t1$get_tidy_schema("table1", v = "latest") # default

We can list files that can be parsed with list_files():

(lf <- t1$list_files())
## # A tibble: 12 × 9
##    tool_parser  parser bname                     size lastmodified        path  pattern prefix group
##    <glue>       <chr>  <chr>                    <fs:> <dttm>              <chr> <chr>   <glue> <glu>
##  1 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv    93 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  2 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   113 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  3 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   133 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _3   
##  4 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    47 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  5 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    70 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  6 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    48 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  7 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    83 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  8 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    34 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  9 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    52 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
## 10 tool1_table5 table5 sampleA.tool1.table5.csv   994 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 11 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv    78 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 12 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv   100 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2
lf |> dplyr::slice(1) |> str()
## tibble [1 × 9] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ tool_parser : 'glue' chr "tool1_table1"
##  $ parser      : chr "table1"
##  $ bname       : chr "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv"
##  $ size        : 'fs_bytes' num 93
##  $ lastmodified: POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-05-15 02:17:48"
##  $ path        : chr "/home/runner/miniconda3/envs/pkgdown_env/lib/R/library/nemo/extdata/tool1/v4.5.6/sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv"
##  $ pattern     : chr "\\.tool1\\.table1\\.tsv$"
##  $ prefix      : 'glue' chr "sampleA"
##  $ group       : 'glue' chr ""

We can parse and tidy files of interest using the tidy function. Note that this function is called on the object and not assigned anywhere:

# this will create a new field tbls containing the tidy data (and optionally
# the 'raw' parsed data)
t1$tidy(tidy = TRUE, keep_raw = TRUE)
t1$tbls
## # A tibble: 12 × 11
##    tool_parser  parser bname   size lastmodified        path  pattern prefix group raw      tidy    
##    <glue>       <chr>  <chr>  <fs:> <dttm>              <chr> <chr>   <glue> <glu> <list>   <list>  
##  1 tool1_table1 table1 sampl…    93 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…       <tibble> <tibble>
##  2 tool1_table1 table1 sampl…   113 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2    <tibble> <tibble>
##  3 tool1_table1 table1 sampl…   133 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _3    <tibble> <tibble>
##  4 tool1_table2 table2 sampl…    47 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…       <tibble> <tibble>
##  5 tool1_table2 table2 sampl…    70 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2    <tibble> <tibble>
##  6 tool1_table3 table3 sampl…    48 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…       <tibble> <tibble>
##  7 tool1_table3 table3 sampl…    83 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2    <tibble> <tibble>
##  8 tool1_table4 table4 sampl…    34 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…       <tibble> <tibble>
##  9 tool1_table4 table4 sampl…    52 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2    <tibble> <tibble>
## 10 tool1_table5 table5 sampl…   994 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…       <tibble> <tibble>
## 11 tool1_table6 table6 sampl…    78 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…       <tibble> <tibble>
## 12 tool1_table6 table6 sampl…   100 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2    <tibble> <tibble>
t1$tbls$raw[[1]] |> dplyr::glimpse()
## Rows: 3
## Columns: 4
## $ SampleID   <chr> "sampleA", "sampleA", "sampleA"
## $ Chromosome <chr> "chr1", "chr2", "chr3"
## $ Start      <int> 10, 100, 1000
## $ End        <int> 50, 500, 5000
# the tidy tibbles are nested to allow for more than one tidy tibble per file
t1$tbls$tidy[[1]][["data"]][[1]] |> dplyr::glimpse()
## Rows: 3
## Columns: 4
## $ sample_id  <chr> "sampleA", "sampleA", "sampleA"
## $ chromosome <chr> "chr1", "chr2", "chr3"
## $ start      <int> 10, 100, 1000
## $ end        <int> 50, 500, 5000

We can also focus on a subset of files to tidy using the filter_files() function. The include and exclude arguments can specify which tool_parsers to include or exclude in the analysis:

# create new Tool1 object
t2 <- nemo::Tool1$new(path = tool1_path)
t2$files
## # A tibble: 12 × 9
##    tool_parser  parser bname                     size lastmodified        path  pattern prefix group
##    <glue>       <chr>  <chr>                    <fs:> <dttm>              <chr> <chr>   <glue> <glu>
##  1 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv    93 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  2 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   113 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  3 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   133 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _3   
##  4 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    47 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  5 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    70 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  6 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    48 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  7 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    83 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  8 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    34 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  9 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    52 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
## 10 tool1_table5 table5 sampleA.tool1.table5.csv   994 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 11 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv    78 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 12 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv   100 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2
t2$filter_files(include = c("tool1_table2", "tool1_table3"))
t2$files
## # A tibble: 4 × 9
##   tool_parser  parser bname                      size lastmodified        path  pattern prefix group
##   <glue>       <chr>  <chr>                    <fs::> <dttm>              <chr> <chr>   <glue> <glu>
## 1 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv     47 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 2 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv     70 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
## 3 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv     48 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 4 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv     83 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2

After tidying the data of interest, we can write the tidy tibbles to various formats, like Apache Parquet, PostgreSQL, CSV/TSV and R’s RDS. Below we can see that the input_id specified is added to the written files in an additional input_id column. This can be used e.g. to distinguish results from different runs in a data pipeline. An optional output_id column can also be added, and an input_pfix column can be included via pfix_include = TRUE.

t2$tidy() # first need to tidy
outdir1 <- tempdir()
fmt <- "csv"
t2$write(diro = outdir1, format = fmt, input_id = "run123")
wfiles <- fs::dir_info(outdir1) |> dplyr::select(1:5)
wfiles |>
  dplyr::mutate(bname = basename(.data$path)) |>
  dplyr::select("bname", "size", "type")
## # A tibble: 18 × 3
##    bname                                 size type 
##    <chr>                          <fs::bytes> <fct>
##  1 file191511e7e353                     4.71K file 
##  2 file19151a97ed75                     4.71K file 
##  3 file19151fc9242a                     4.71K file 
##  4 file19153c415073                     4.71K file 
##  5 file191542579290                     4.71K file 
##  6 file1915456033d9                     4.71K file 
##  7 file19154aa6302c                     4.71K file 
##  8 file19154d593ff8                     4.71K file 
##  9 file1915524bf4f9                     4.71K file 
## 10 file19155aab1748                     4.71K file 
## 11 file19155f82646d                     4.71K file 
## 12 file19157bebcd1b                     4.71K file 
## 13 file19159267916                      4.71K file 
## 14 rmarkdown-str1915793ae556.html       1.13K file 
## 15 sampleA_2_tool1_table2.csv.gz           81 file 
## 16 sampleA_2_tool1_table3.csv.gz           93 file 
## 17 sampleA_tool1_table2.csv.gz             62 file 
## 18 sampleA_tool1_table3.csv.gz             77 file
# readr::read_csv(wfiles$path[1], show_col_types = F) # see bug #137

The nemofy function is a convenient wrapper for the process of filtering, tidying, and writing.

t3 <- nemo::Tool1$new(path = tool1_path)
outdir2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "t3") |> fs::dir_create()
t3$files
## # A tibble: 12 × 9
##    tool_parser  parser bname                     size lastmodified        path  pattern prefix group
##    <glue>       <chr>  <chr>                    <fs:> <dttm>              <chr> <chr>   <glue> <glu>
##  1 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv    93 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  2 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   113 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  3 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   133 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _3   
##  4 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    47 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  5 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    70 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  6 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    48 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  7 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    83 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  8 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    34 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  9 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    52 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
## 10 tool1_table5 table5 sampleA.tool1.table5.csv   994 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 11 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv    78 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 12 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv   100 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2
t3$nemofy(
  diro = outdir2,
  format = "tsv",
  input_id = "run_t3"
)
wfiles2 <- fs::dir_info(outdir2) |>
  dplyr::mutate(bname = basename(.data$path))
wfiles2 |>
  dplyr::select("bname", "size", "type")
## # A tibble: 12 × 3
##    bname                                size type 
##    <chr>                         <fs::bytes> <fct>
##  1 sampleA_2_tool1_table1.tsv.gz         105 file 
##  2 sampleA_2_tool1_table2.tsv.gz          81 file 
##  3 sampleA_2_tool1_table3.tsv.gz          93 file 
##  4 sampleA_2_tool1_table4.tsv.gz         104 file 
##  5 sampleA_2_tool1_table6.tsv.gz         115 file 
##  6 sampleA_3_tool1_table1.tsv.gz         118 file 
##  7 sampleA_tool1_table1.tsv.gz            91 file 
##  8 sampleA_tool1_table2.tsv.gz            62 file 
##  9 sampleA_tool1_table3.tsv.gz            77 file 
## 10 sampleA_tool1_table4.tsv.gz            79 file 
## 11 sampleA_tool1_table5.tsv.gz           209 file 
## 12 sampleA_tool1_table6.tsv.gz            98 file
readr::read_tsv(wfiles2$path[2], show_col_types = F)
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   input_id sample_id metric_a metric_b
##   <chr>    <chr>     <chr>       <dbl>
## 1 run_t3   sampleA   a           12.3 
## 2 run_t3   sampleA   b            4.56
## 3 run_t3   sampleA   c            7.89

Workflow

A Workflow consists of a list of one or more Tools. We can construct a certain Workflow with different Tools, which would allow parsing and writing tidy tables from a variety of bioinformatic tools. See ?Workflow.

For example, {nemo} contains a Workflow1 class as a Workflow child (containing only a single Tool1 for simplicity). Similarly to Tool, a Workflow object contains functions such as filter_files, list_files, tidy, write and nemofy:

w <- system.file("extdata/tool1", package = "nemo") |>
  nemo::Workflow1$new()
outdir3 <- file.path(tempdir(), "oa") |> fs::dir_create()
w$list_files()
## # A tibble: 12 × 9
##    tool_parser  parser bname                     size lastmodified        path  pattern prefix group
##    <glue>       <chr>  <chr>                    <fs:> <dttm>              <chr> <chr>   <glue> <glu>
##  1 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv    93 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  2 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   113 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  3 tool1_table1 table1 sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv   133 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _3   
##  4 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    47 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  5 tool1_table2 table2 sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv    70 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  6 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    48 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  7 tool1_table3 table3 sampleA.tool1.table3.tsv    83 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
##  8 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    34 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
##  9 tool1_table4 table4 sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv    52 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2   
## 10 tool1_table5 table5 sampleA.tool1.table5.csv   994 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 11 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv    78 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl…      
## 12 tool1_table6 table6 sampleA.tool1.table6.csv   100 2026-05-15 02:17:48 /hom… "\\.to… sampl… _2
x <- w$nemofy(
  diro = outdir3,
  format = "tsv",
  input_id = "workflow1_run1"
)
wfiles3 <- fs::dir_info(outdir3) |>
  dplyr::select(1:5) |>
  dplyr::mutate(bname = basename(.data$path))
wfiles3 |>
  dplyr::select("bname", "size", "type")
## # A tibble: 13 × 3
##    bname                                size type     
##    <chr>                         <fs::bytes> <fct>    
##  1 _metadata                              4K directory
##  2 sampleA_2_tool1_table1.tsv.gz         112 file     
##  3 sampleA_2_tool1_table2.tsv.gz          89 file     
##  4 sampleA_2_tool1_table3.tsv.gz         101 file     
##  5 sampleA_2_tool1_table4.tsv.gz         110 file     
##  6 sampleA_2_tool1_table6.tsv.gz         126 file     
##  7 sampleA_3_tool1_table1.tsv.gz         124 file     
##  8 sampleA_tool1_table1.tsv.gz            98 file     
##  9 sampleA_tool1_table2.tsv.gz            71 file     
## 10 sampleA_tool1_table3.tsv.gz            85 file     
## 11 sampleA_tool1_table4.tsv.gz            87 file     
## 12 sampleA_tool1_table5.tsv.gz           217 file     
## 13 sampleA_tool1_table6.tsv.gz           108 file
